title: Coelachyrum lagopoides
intro: Coelachyrum lagopoides, also known as Paterson's grass or Lagopoid grass, is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Coelachyrum lagopoides has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Coelachyrum lagopoides include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Coelachyrum lagopoides is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and it is also used in the production of capsules and extracts for respiratory health support.
taxonomy: Coelachyrum lagopoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Poeae and the genus Coelachyrum. Coelachyrum lagopoides is native to Australia, where it can be found in various regions including Western Australia and the Northern Territory. The classification of Coelachyrum lagopoides is further broken down into the family level, Poaceae, which is also known as the grass family. Within the family Poaceae, Coelachyrum lagopoides is classified as a monocotyledonous plant.
